query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "alayaworld: long-horizon and playable video world generation" computer science
rationale: This paper belongs in the literature review as it directly addresses the core mechanism of "extending" video world models to multi-agent scenarios, which is the specific follow-up direction requested for the "AlayaWorld" work. It satisfies criteria (a) and (d) by providing the canonical methodology and empirical evidence for multi-agent video generation, a necessary component for any review discussing the extension of single-observer world models like AlayaWorld.
